questions.question: Find the area of a segment of a circle of radius 14 cm and angle 90°.
questions.answer: Area of sector = (90/360) × π × 14² = 49π cm². Area of triangle = (1/2) × 14 × 14 = 98 cm². Area of segment = 49π − 98 cm².

questions.question: Find the area of a major sector if the minor sector angle is 60° and radius is r.
questions.answer: Major sector angle = 360° − 60° = 300°. Area = (300/360) × πr² = (5/6)πr².

questions.question: Explain with steps how trigonometry is used in finding the area of a segment.
questions.answer: Trigonometry helps find the height of the triangle formed inside the sector using sine or cosine ratios, which is required to calculate the triangle’s area.

questions.question: Find the area of a sector of radius 21 cm and angle 120°.
Find the area of a circle whose circumference is 44 cm.
questions.answer: 2πr = 44 ⇒ r = 7 cm. Area = π × 7² = 49π cm².

questions.question: Explain why the area of a sector is proportional to its angle.
questions.answer: Since the area of the full circle corresponds to 360°, the area of a sector depends directly on the central angle.

questions.question: Find the arc length of a sector of radius 28 cm and angle 45°.
questions.answer: Arc length = (45/360) × 2π × 28 = 7π cm.

questions.question: Explain why the area of a segment can never exceed the area of the circle.
questions.answer: A segment is only a part of the circle and hence its area is always less than the total area of the circle.

questions.question: Find the area of a quadrant if radius is r.
questions.answer: Area of quadrant = (1/4) × πr².

questions.question: Explain why the angle at the centre is used in all sector formulas.
questions.answer: The central angle determines the fraction of the circle taken by the sector.

questions.question: Find the area of a semicircle of radius 14 cm.
questions.answer: Area = (1/2) × π × 14² = 98π cm².

questions.question: Explain why the arc length increases when radius increases.
questions.answer: Arc length is directly proportional to the radius, so increasing radius increases arc length.

questions.question: Find the area of a sector when θ = 180°.
Explain why the area of a circle with radius zero is zero.
questions.answer: With zero radius, there is no enclosed region, hence area is zero.

questions.question: Explain the importance of mensuration in daily life using circles.
questions.answer: Mensuration helps calculate areas and lengths in objects like wheels, clocks, and fields.

questions.question: Find the area of a circle if r = 21 cm.
questions.answer: Area = π × 21² = 441π cm².

questions.question: Explain why the triangle formed in a segment helps find its area.
questions.answer: The triangle area is subtracted from the sector area to obtain the segment area.

questions.question: Find the arc length of a full circle of radius r.
questions.answer: Arc length = 2πr.

questions.question: Explain why the sum of angles around a point is 360°.
questions.answer: A complete rotation around a point forms a full circle measuring 360°.

questions.question: Find the area of a major sector if the minor sector angle is θ.
questions.answer: Major sector angle = 360° − θ. Area = ((360 − θ)/360) × πr².

questions.question: Explain why sector area becomes zero when θ = 0°.
questions.answer: When θ = 0°, no region is enclosed, so the area is zero.

questions.question: Find the area of a quadrant when diameter is 14 cm.
questions.answer: Radius = 7 cm. Area = (1/4) × π × 7² = (49π)/4 cm².

questions.question: Explain why the value of π is the same for all circles.
questions.answer: π is the ratio of circumference to diameter and remains constant for all circles.

questions.question: Explain the difference between arc length and circumference.
questions.answer: Circumference is the total boundary of a circle, while arc length is a part of it.

questions.question: Summarise all important formulas of areas related to circles.
questions.answer: Important formulas include: area of circle = πr², circumference = 2πr, area of sector = (θ/360)πr², arc length = (θ/360)2πr, area of segment = sector area − triangle area.
